summary:
I've compiled the review report at `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/review-report-writer/output/review_report.md`.

To produce it, I read the two Review-phase critics' verdicts (requirements-conformance-critic: **pass** on all 50 requirements; code-quality-critic: **needs_work** on performance/quality only, with no correctness or security defects), pulled in the Build-phase test-critic verdict (**pass** — 213 tests/738 assertions green, rubocop clean, with system tests blocked only by a local chromedriver/Chrome version mismatch), grounded "what was asked" in the Define requirements (R1–R50) and "what was built" in the implementer's summary and the actual git diff (app +703/−29, tests +552/−5, docs/guides +22/−1). The resulting PR-ready report covers: a top-line status, the original ask, an inventory of what shipped (three query objects, a per-user `Dashboard::Broadcast` service across 8 call sites, the home view/partial tree, three Stimulus controllers, and the answer-questions modal flow), per-critic evidence of conformance, a table of the four open findings (F1–F4, two major scalability + two minor consistency issues, each with location and suggested fix), and a merge recommendation with the F1/F2 performance work and CI system-test check flagged as fast-follows.
